regions.us-ports: Today, northeast winds will remain moderate across most US ports with no marine wind warnings in effect. Charleston and nearby Southeast ports can expect clear skies and seas under 3 feet, providing smooth conditions for fishing and yacht operations. Along the Gulf Coast, including Galveston and Houston, light to moderate northeast breezes and calm seas support safe docking and departure. West Coast ports such as Long Beach and San Francisco will see mild conditions with light winds and minimal swell, ideal for passenger embarkation and small craft activity.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today, expect light rain showers with ESE winds steady at 12 knots and seas around 2 feet across the Bahamas and Central Caribbean. Conditions will remain generally stable for navigation between Nassau, Freeport, Bimini, and Grand Cayman, though limited visibility and wet decks warrant caution for yacht and fishing captains. Moderate rain is forecast for tomorrow, especially near Puerto Plata and Santo Domingo, so plan for reduced visibility and potential delays in those ports. Mariners should monitor seas, which will remain below Small Craft Advisory thresholds, but remain vigilant for localized showers impacting anchorage comfort in Berry Islands and Key West.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today, southeast winds will increase to 13 knots across the Eastern Caribbean, with seas remaining moderate near 4 feet and a gentle northeast swell at 3.5 feet every 7 seconds. Expect sunny skies around San Juan, St. Thomas, St. Croix, St. Maarten, St. Kitts, and Antigua, with no precipitation or marine warnings in effect. Cruise and yacht captains should maintain standard vigilance for moderate seas when approaching anchorages, especially in exposed areas like St. Maarten’s Great Bay. Fishing operations can proceed safely with steady trade winds and calm waters, but monitor updates for any changes in swell or wind speed.
regions.western-caribbean: Today conditions across the Western Caribbean are generally calm with light northwest winds 4 to 6 knots and seas under 1 foot, providing smooth navigation for all vessels. Expect sunny skies at Cozumel with excellent visibility, though mist and fog may reduce visibility near Costa Maya and Belize City over the next two days—exercise caution when maneuvering in port approaches. Fishing captains should plan for stable waters but remain vigilant for localized fog on April 9, which could impact early morning operations. No marine warnings are in effect; anchorage conditions remain favorable throughout the region.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today, easterly winds remain steady at 22 to 24 knots across the Southern Caribbean with patchy rain near Aruba and Bonaire but no significant precipitation expected. Seas are moderate, running 4.6 to 4.9 feet with a northeast swell of 3.1 feet at 6 seconds, posing no small craft advisory conditions. Captains should maintain caution when maneuvering near Cartagena and Curacao where localized showers could reduce visibility briefly. Passengers are advised to secure loose items on deck and expect some intermittent moisture during outdoor activities.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today, easterly winds at 17 knots will maintain moderate seas near 5.4 feet with a 3.6-foot easterly swell at 6 seconds, posing no Small Craft Advisory conditions across the Lesser Antilles. Patchy rain near Barbados and Dominica may reduce visibility briefly; yacht and fishing captains should secure loose gear and monitor radar for localized showers. Ports including St. Lucia, Martinique, and St. Maarten will experience mainly dry conditions with steady trade winds favorable for navigation. Mariners should expect stable sea states through April 9 with light precipitation returning intermittently, ensuring routine watchkeeping and caution in anchorage approaches.
regions.los-angeles: Today conditions across Los Angeles, including Los Angeles / San Pedro, Long Beach, Marina del Rey, and Catalina Island, are calm with SSE winds 6 to 8 knots and seas around 3 to 4 feet, ideal for all marine operations. Expect sunny skies with no precipitation, making it favorable for passenger activities and fishing trips. Over the next two days, winds will increase slightly to SSE 9-12 knots with overcast skies but remain well below Small Craft Advisory thresholds. Vessel operators should maintain routine anchorage checks in Catalina and monitor visibility changes during overcast conditions.
regions.ensenada: Today, light northerly winds 8 knots and sunny skies will prevail across Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos with seas remaining calm near 3.8 feet. Mariners can expect comfortable conditions for transit and anchorage with swell from WNW at 2.7 feet and 5-second periods presenting minimal motion. Fishing captains should plan for stable waters early in the day, while yacht operators should maintain vigilance for typical coastal sea breezes developing by afternoon. No marine warnings are in effect; standard docking and tender operations at Ensenada and Punta Banda ports are advised.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Today, steady NNW winds at 11 knots will prevail across Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz with mostly sunny skies and calm seas under 5 feet. Mariners can expect smooth navigation conditions with a mild swell from the south, making anchorages in Cabo San Lucas and San Jose del Cabo secure and comfortable for overnight stays. Fishing captains should take advantage of the stable weather and clear visibility, while yacht operators should maintain routine watch for any shifts in wind speed. No precipitation or advisories are expected through the day, supporting safe and pleasant cruising operations.
regions.mazatlan: Today, expect easterly winds of 9 knots under overcast skies across Mazatlan and Topolobampo with no precipitation. Seas remain moderate, running 3 to 4 feet with a gentle 2.7-foot swell from the south-southwest at 11 seconds, producing comfortable conditions for small craft operations. Yacht and fishing captains should maintain a cautious approach when navigating nearshore, especially in Topolobampo harbor where shifting winds may affect anchorage holding. Passengers can anticipate smooth cruising with limited cloud cover improvement over the next two days.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Today, east winds will increase from 2 to around 9 knots across Puerto Vallarta, Manzanillo, Huatulco, and Zihuatanejo, with seas remaining calm at 1.4 to 2.0 feet. Skippers can expect partly cloudy skies and no precipitation, providing stable conditions for navigation and fishing. Anchorage in Puerto Vallarta remains secure with light swell from the southwest; small craft operators should maintain routine vigilance but no advisories are in effect. Expect continued mild easterly winds through April 9 with improving sunshine, ideal for cruise and yacht operations.
regions.western-mediterranean: Today the Western Mediterranean is experiencing light WNW winds 2 to 5 knots with sunny skies and calm seas under 1.5 feet, ideal for smooth cruising and safe yacht operations. Ports including Barcelona, Valencia, Palma de Mallorca, Marseille, Genoa, Civitavecchia, Naples, and Palermo will see stable, dry conditions with no precipitation expected. Mariners should note the gentle swell from the SSE at 0.5 feet and maintain routine anchorage checks, especially in Palma de Mallorca where light winds favor safe holding. Expect similar conditions tomorrow with a slight increase in WNW winds up to 8 knots, easing again by April 9 when overcast skies develop but seas remain below Small Craft Advisory thresholds.
regions.central-mediterranean: Today conditions across the Central Mediterranean from Malta to Dubrovnik are stable with westerly winds around 8-10 knots and seas under 1.5 feet, supporting smooth navigation and comfortable cruising. Sunny skies and low swell will favor anchoring and tender operations at ports including Cagliari, Messina, and Bari. Yacht and fishing captains can expect calm seas and steady winds, ideal for offshore activities; standard vigilance for localized gusts near coastal topography is advised. Passengers should enjoy clear weather but remain hydrated when ashore, especially in exposed ports like Tunis and Split.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today, expect light WNW winds at 6 knots and sunny skies across the Eastern Mediterranean, with calm seas under 1 foot ideal for smooth navigation near Athens, Piraeus, Santorini, and Mykonos. Yacht captains should note increasing WNW winds up to 17 knots tomorrow, requiring careful sail trim and monitoring near exposed anchorages like Rhodes and Kusadasi. Fishing operations can proceed with minimal interference today, but prepare for patchy rain and light precipitation approaching by April 9 around Istanbul and Corfu. No marine warnings are in effect, but remain vigilant for localized showers later in the week.
